title: Mahakali Chalisa
intro: Shri Mahakali Chalisa: Full Path, Meaning, and Benefits - Hymn to Goddess Mahakali
description:
|| Shri Mahakali Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Jai jai Sita Ram ke
Madhyavasini Amba
Dehu darsh Jagadamba
Ab karo na Matu vilamba
Jai Tara jai Kalika
Jai dash vidya vrund
Kali Chalisa rachat
Ek siddhi kavi Hind
Pratah kaal uth jo padhe
Dupahariya ya shaam
Duhkh daridrata door hon
Siddhi hoy sab kaam

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Kali Kankal Malini
Jai Mangala Maha Kapalini
Raktbeej badhkarini Mata
Sada bhakt jananki sukhdata
Shiro malika bhushit ange
Jai Kali jai madya matange
Har hridayarvind suvilasini
Jai Jagadamba sakal duhkh nashini
Hreem Kali Shri Mahakali
Kreem Kalyani Dakshinakali
Jai Kalavati jai Vidyavati
Jai Tara Sundari mahamati
Dehu subuddhi harahu sab sankat
Hohu bhakt ke aage pargat
Jai Om kare jai hunkare
Maha shakti jai aparampare
Kamala kaliyug darp vinashini
Sada bhakt jan ke bhaynashini
Ab Jagadamba na der lagavahu
Duhkh daridrata mor hatavahu
Jayati karal Kalika Mata
Kalanl saman dyutigata
Jaishankari Sureshi sanatani
Koti siddhi kavi Matu puratani
Kapardini kali kalp bimochni
Jai viksit nav nalin vilochni
Anand karani anand nidhana
Dehu Matu mohi nirmal gyana
Karunamrit sagar kripamayi
Hohu dusht janpar ab nirdayi
Sakal jeev tohi param piyara
Sakal vishwa tore adhara
Pralay kaal mein nartan karini
Jai janani sab jag ki palani
Mahodari Maheshwari Maya
Himgiri suta vishwa ki chhaya
Svachhand rad marad dhuni mahi
Garjat tumhi aur kou nahi
Sphurti maniganakar pratane
Taragan tu byom vitane
Shri dhare santan hitkarini
Agni pani ati dusht vidarini
Dhumra vilochni pran vimochni
Shumbh Nishumbh mathani varlochni
Sahas bhuji saroruh malini
Chamunde maraghat ki vasini
Khappar madhya sushonit saaji
Marehu maa Mahishasur paaji
Amb Ambika Chand Chandika
Sab eke tum Aadi Kalika
Aja ekrupa bahurupa
Akath charitra tav shakti anupa
Calcutta ke dakshin dware
Murti tor Maheshi apare
Kadambari panrat Shyama
Jai Matangi kaam ke dhama
Kamlasan vasini Kamalayani
Jai Shyama jai jai Shyamayani
Matangi jai jayati prakriti he
Jayati bhakti ur kumati sumati he
Kotibrahma Shiv Vishnu kamada
Jayati ahinsa dharm janmada
Jal thal nabhmandal mein vyapini
Saudamini madhya alapini
Jhanan tachhu maririn nadini
Jai Saraswati Veena vadini
Om Aim Hreem Kleem Chamundayai Vicche
Kalit kanth shobhit narmunda
Jai brahmand siddhi kavi Mata
Kamakhya aur Kali Mata
Hinglaj Vindhyachal vasini
Attahasini aru aghan nashini
Kitni stuti karun akhande
Tu brahmande shaktijitchande
Karahu kripa sabpe Jagadamba
Rahahin nishank tor avalamba
Chaturbhuji Kali tum Shyama
Roop tumhar maha abhirama
Khadg aur khappar kar sohat
Sur nar muni sabko man mohat
Tumhari kripa pave jo koi
Rog shok nahin takahun hoi
Jo yah path kare Chalisa
Taapar kripa karahi Gaurisha

|| Doha ||
Jai Kapalini jai Shiva
Jai jai jai Jagadamba
Sada bhaktjan keri duhkh harahu
Matu avalamba
meaning:
|| Shri Mahakali Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Jai jai Sita Ram ke
(Victory to the worshippers of Sita-Ram)
Madhyavasini Amba
(O Mother who dwells in the middle, victory to you)
Dehu darsh Jagadamba
(O Mother of the Universe, grant me a vision)
Ab karo na Matu vilamba
(O Mother, do not delay any longer)
Jai Tara jai Kalika
(Victory to Tara, victory to Kalika)
Jai dash vidya vrund
(Victory to the group of ten Mahavidyas)
Kali Chalisa rachat
(This Kali Chalisa is being composed)
Ek siddhi kavi Hind
(By a poet who seeks spiritual accomplishment)
Pratah kaal uth jo padhe
(Whoever reads this rising in the morning)
Dupahariya ya shaam
(Or at noon or in the evening)
Duhkh daridrata door hon
(Their sorrows and poverty are removed)
Siddhi hoy sab kaam
(And all their works are accomplished)

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Kali Kankal Malini
(Victory to Kali, the wearer of the garland of skulls)
Jai Mangala Maha Kapalini
(Victory to the auspicious and great Kapalini)
Raktbeej badhkarini Mata
(O Mother, slayer of Raktbeej)
Sada bhakt jananki sukhdata
(Always the giver of happiness to your devotees)
Shiro malika bhushit ange
(Whose body is adorned with a garland of heads)
Jai Kali jai madya matange
(Victory to Kali, who is intoxicated with divine energy)
Har hridayarvind suvilasini
(Who dwells beautifully in the lotus-heart of Shiva)
Jai Jagadamba sakal duhkh nashini
(Victory to Jagadamba, the destroyer of all sorrows)
Hreem Kali Shri Mahakali
(Hreem Kali, Shri Mahakali)
Kreem Kalyani Dakshinakali
(Kreem Kalyani, Dakshinakali)
Jai Kalavati jai Vidyavati
(Victory to Kalavati, victory to Vidyavati)
Jai Tara Sundari mahamati
(Victory to the wise and beautiful Tara)
Dehu subuddhi harahu sab sankat
(Grant me pure wisdom and remove all crises)
Hohu bhakt ke aage pargat
(Appear before your devotee)
Jai Om kare jai hunkare
(Victory to the Om-kar, victory to the one who roars)
Maha shakti jai aparampare
(Victory to the infinite Great Power)
Kamala kaliyug darp vinashini
(O Kamala, destroyer of the pride of the Kaliyuga)
Sada bhakt jan ke bhaynashini
(Always the destroyer of the fear of devotees)
Ab Jagadamba na der lagavahu
(O Jagadamba, do not delay now)
Duhkh daridrata mor hatavahu
(Remove my sorrows and poverty)
Jayati karal Kalika Mata
(Victory to the fierce Mother Kalika)
Kalanl saman dyutigata
(Whose radiance is like the fire of destruction)
Jaishankari Sureshi sanatani
(Victory to Shankari, the Goddess of gods, the Eternal one)
Koti siddhi kavi Matu puratani
(O primordial Mother, granter of millions of siddhis)
Kapardini kali kalp bimochni
(O Kapardini, liberator from the suffering of the age)
Jai viksit nav nalin vilochni
(Victory to the one with eyes like a blooming lotus)
Anand karani anand nidhana
(O giver of bliss, O treasure of bliss)
Dehu Matu mohi nirmal gyana
(O Mother, grant me pure knowledge)
Karunamrit sagar kripamayi
(O Ocean of nectar of compassion, O merciful one)
Hohu dusht janpar ab nirdayi
(Be merciless now towards the wicked)
Sakal jeev tohi param piyara
(All living beings are very dear to you)
Sakal vishwa tore adhara
(The entire universe is supported by you)
Pralay kaal mein nartan karini
(The dancer at the time of dissolution)
Jai janani sab jag ki palani
(Victory to the Mother, the protector of the world)
Mahodari Maheshwari Maya
(O Mahodari, Maheshwari, and Maya)
Himgiri suta vishwa ki chhaya
(O daughter of the Himalayas, the shadow of the universe)
Svachhand rad marad dhuni mahi
(Your sound echoes everywhere)
Garjat tumhi aur kou nahi
(You alone roar, no one else does)
Sphurti maniganakar pratane
(You shine like a cluster of jewels)
Taragan tu byom vitane
(You are the stars in the vast sky)
Shri dhare santan hitkarini
(O bringer of prosperity, the well-wisher of saints)
Agni pani ati dusht vidarini
(With fire in your hand, you tear apart the wicked)
Dhumra vilochni pran vimochni
(Slayer of Dhumralochan, liberator of souls)
Shumbh Nishumbh mathani varlochni
(Destroyer of Shumbh and Nishumbh, the beautiful-eyed one)
Sahas bhuji saroruh malini
(O thousand-armed one, wearer of lotus garlands)
Chamunde maraghat ki vasini
(O Chamunda, dweller of the cremation ground)
Khappar madhya sushonit saaji
(Adorned with blood in your skull-bowl)
Marehu maa Mahishasur paaji
(O Mother, you killed the wicked Mahishasura)
Amb Ambika Chand Chandika
(O Amb, Ambika, Chand, Chandika)
Sab eke tum Aadi Kalika
(You are all one, O Primordial Kalika)
Aja ekrupa bahurupa
(O Unborn, O One-formed, O Multi-formed)
Akath charitra tav shakti anupa
(Indescribable is your character and peerless your power)
Calcutta ke dakshin dware
(At the southern gate of Calcutta)
Murti tor Maheshi apare
(O Maheshi, your idol is immense)
Kadambari panrat Shyama
(O Shyama, who is delighted in divine intoxication)
Jai Matangi kaam ke dhama
(Victory to Matangi, the source of all desires)
Kamlasan vasini Kamalayani
(O dweller of the lotus throne, O Kamala)
Jai Shyama jai jai Shyamayani
(Victory to Shyama, victory to Shyamayani)
Matangi jai jayati prakriti he
(Victory to Matangi, you are the nature itself)
Jayati bhakti ur kumati sumati he
(Victory to the giver of devotion, turner of bad mind to good)
Kotibrahma Shiv Vishnu kamada
(Granter of desires to Brahma, Shiva, and Vishnu)
Jayati ahinsa dharm janmada
(Victory to the mother of non-violence and dharma)
Jal thal nabhmandal mein vyapini
(Pervading water, earth, and the sky)
Saudamini madhya alapini
(Resonating like a bolt of lightning)
Jhanan tachhu maririn nadini
(Whose sound is like the ringing of bells)
Jai Saraswati Veena vadini
(Victory to Saraswati, the Veena player)
Om Aim Hreem Kleem Chamundayai Vicche
(Victory to the mantra: Om Aim Hreem Kleem Chamundayai Vicche)
Kalit kanth shobhit narmunda
(Whose neck is adorned with human skulls)
Jai brahmand siddhi kavi Mata
(Victory to the Mother who grants siddhis to the universe)
Kamakhya aur Kali Mata
(O Kamakhya and Kali Mata)
Hinglaj Vindhyachal vasini
(O Dweller of Hinglaj and Vindhyachal)
Attahasini aru aghan nashini
(O loud-laugher and destroyer of sins)
Kitni stuti karun akhande
(O Indivisible One, how much can I praise you)
Tu brahmande shaktijitchande
(You are the power and conqueror of the universe)
Karahu kripa sabpe Jagadamba
(O Jagadamba, bestow your grace upon all)
Rahahin nishank tor avalamba
(Those who rely on you live without fear)
Chaturbhuji Kali tum Shyama
(O Four-armed Kali, O Shyama)
Roop tumhar maha abhirama
(Your form is extremely beautiful)
Khadg aur khappar kar sohat
(The sword and skull-bowl in your hands look beautiful)
Sur nar muni sabko man mohat
(You enchant the minds of gods, humans, and sages)
Tumhari kripa pave jo koi
(Whoever receives your grace)
Rog shok nahin takahun hoi
(They suffer no disease or sorrow)
Jo yah path kare Chalisa
(Whoever recites this Chalisa)
Taapar kripa karahi Gaurisha
(Gaurisha [Shiva] bestows his grace upon them)

|| Doha ||
Jai Kapalini jai Shiva
(Victory to Kapalini, victory to Shiva)
Jai jai jai Jagadamba
(Victory, victory, victory to Jagadamba)
Sada bhaktjan keri duhkh harahu
(Always remove the sorrows of your devotees)
Matu avalamba
(O Mother, you are our only support)

faq.q1: Why is the Mahakali Chalisa recited?
faq.a1: Maa Kali is considered the deity of time and the destroyer of evil. Reciting this Chalisa is done to destroy ego, ignorance, fear, and negative energies within the mind.
faq.q2: What is the most auspicious time and day to recite it?
faq.a2: The night of Amavasya (New Moon) and Tuesdays or Saturdays are considered extremely powerful and effective days for the worship of Mahakali and the recitation of her Chalisa.
faq.q3: Should one be afraid of worshiping Mahakali?
faq.a3: Absolutely not. Maa Kali is a loving mother to her devotees. She only destroys evil. Reciting the Chalisa removes all kinds of fears from the mind.
faq.q4: Is any special initiation required to recite the Mahakali Chalisa?
faq.a4: No, Mahakali is a deity of a simple heart. You can recite it with full devotion, purity, and faith without any complex rituals.
faq.q5: What are the benefits of its regular recitation?
faq.a5: Regular recitation grants immense courage, self-confidence, and inner strength to the individual. It provides the fortitude to fight against the difficult situations of life.
